description: The bird's song was the classic three-syllable rrRH-BEE-uh of Alder Flycatcher, very harsh and heavily modulated. Each time, I heard the three notes, the first one creaky and grinding, the second one explosive, the third one relatively faint and fading.  I saw the bird very briefly in a tall sapling, and could only tell that it was an Empidonax flycatcher. Then I lost it. Despite lengthy searching, I and my companion (David Gillilan) could not re-find it. The trees around the ranch house are very dense, and birds often get holed up in there for long periods of time.Behaviors: Other than hearing it sing, no behaviors noted. The bird was briefly seen near the top of a tall sapling; then it flew away, possibly staying within the grove.Call: This was the basis for the ID. The bird was singing its rrRH-BEE-uh song in the productive grove of trees near the Fox Ranch headquarters. It sang about 5 times, then disappeared for good.Plumage: presumed adult
